figures.caption: TABLE I: Overview of the linear system and quadratic program used to compute a hyperplane between robot R and obstacle O, with Nd = No +1. α is linearly related to the classification error of the hyperplane classifier, Γ represents the labels of the robot and obstacle class.

TABLE III: Summary of the results up to eight obstacles: (1, 2) the maximum computation time added by solving the LS or QP, (3) the median computation time to solve Eq. (6), (4) the median number of iterations to solve Eq. (6), (5) the median and standard deviation of the error on trajectory time, (6) the success rate and (7) the reduction in computation time from LSR to the decoupled method. All reported maximum and median numbers are computed over all successful trajectories per No.
Fig. 2: Results of the comparison between the coupled and decoupled approach in terms of (top) computation time per iteration and (bottom) total computation time to solve Eq. (6) per No.
